title: FIR Filter Kernel: Minimal-Multiplication Schedule for a Fixed Tap Polynomial
strategies: Horner's rule over the dense taps: exactly d multiplications (trivial, = baseline)
recover the hidden quadratic-section power and raise it by naive repeated multiply: k+1 mults (greedy)
recover the section and raise it with a repeated-squaring addition chain: ~2+log2(k) mults (strong)
shortest addition chain for the exponent / joint sharing below binary exponentiation (open ceiling, uncovered)
